description_long: This is a robust, double-headed mallet with a cylindrical head and a long, straight handle. The head is thick and symmetrical, lacking any claws or peen, and appears to be made from a dense, solid material. The handle is slender, slightly contoured for grip, and tapers gently towards the end, with a rounded knob at the base for secure handling. The entire object is a uniform dark gray, suggesting it is either painted or made from a single material. There are no visible markings, logos, or decorative elements on the surface. The hammer appears clean and free from any visible wear, damage, or attachments.
description: A solid, double-headed mallet with a cylindrical head and a straight, contoured handle, all in dark gray. The surface is clean and unmarked, with no visible accessories or damage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical head and the straight handle with a rounded knob at the base.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the circular face of the hammer's head and the slim profile of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the opposite side of the cylindrical head and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the circular face of the head and the handle's profile.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a slight contour and a rounded knob at the end, smooth surface.
attribute_head_type: Double-headed cylindrical mallet head, symmetrical with flat striking faces on both ends, no claws or peen.
